title: crotalaria subcalvata
intro: Crotalaria subcalvata is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the bushy rattlebox, characterized by its hairy stems and yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Crotalaria subcalvata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
